learningObjectives: Name and use twelve daily routine words at Movers A1 level.
Use present simple to describe everyday habits and school-day routines.
Place adverbs of frequency (always, sometimes, never) correctly in sentences.
Read a short text about a school day and identify the main idea and key details.
Listen for times and sequence words in short dialogues about routines.
Write a short note or message (at least 15 words) about daily habits.
Talk about your morning and school day in short spoken answers.
grammarFocus: Adverbs of frequency: always, sometimes, never

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I wake up at six thirty every day.
Minh has breakfast with his family.
We have lunch at school at twelve.
My family eats dinner at six o'clock.
I always do my homework after school.
She always brushes her teeth before school.
I sometimes play football after homework.
He never watches TV before homework.
I brush my teeth every morning.
Minh goes to bed at nine o'clock.
I walk to school every day.

grammarReference.explanation: Use present simple for routines and habits that happen regularly. Add -s or -es for he/she/it: He wakes up at seven. Use do/does in questions and negatives.
examples: I wake up at six thirty every day.
She has breakfast with her family.
Minh goes to school at seven thirty.
Do you do your homework after school?
commonMistakes: He wake up at seven (×) → He wakes up at seven (✓)
I am go to school every day (×) → I go to school every day (✓)
She have lunch at school (×) → She has lunch at school (✓)

grammarReference.structure: Adverbs of frequency: always, sometimes, never
grammarReference.explanation: Place always, sometimes and never before the main verb (I always do homework). With be, put the adverb after am/is/are (She is always happy). Never means zero times.
examples: I always brush my teeth in the morning.
He sometimes plays football after school.
She never watches TV before homework.
They are always hungry before dinner.
commonMistakes: I do always homework (×) → I always do homework (✓)
She never is late (×) → She is never late (✓)
Sometimes I am play football (×) → Sometimes I play football (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Morning Routine Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and use wake up, breakfast, get dressed and brush teeth in short sentences.

exercises.title: Learn: Morning Routine Words
exercises.instructions: Read each clue. Choose the best word or phrase.

questions.questionText: At six thirty Minh stops sleeping. He _____.
questions.explanation: Wake up (thức dậy) là bắt đầu ngày mới. Go to bed là đi ngủ.

questions.questionText: The first meal in the morning is _____.
questions.explanation: Breakfast (bữa sáng) là bữa đầu tiên trong ngày.

questions.questionText: After breakfast Minh puts on his school uniform. He _____.
questions.explanation: Get dressed (mặc quần áo) sau khi ăn sáng.

questions.questionText: Minh cleans his teeth in the bathroom. He _____.
questions.explanation: Brush teeth (đánh răng) trong phòng tắm buổi sáng.

exercises.title: Practice: Match Morning Routines
exercises.instructions: Match each phrase to what it means.

questions.questionText: Match the morning routine phrases.
questions.explanation: Mỗi cụm mô tả một hoạt động buổi sáng rõ ràng.

pairs.right: stop sleeping and get out of bed
the first meal of the day

exercises.title: Check: Morning Words Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Mum says: 'Minh, _____! It's six thirty.'
questions.explanation: Wake up! — gọi thức dậy. Go to bed là buổi tối.

questions.questionText: I always have rice and eggs for _____.
questions.explanation: Rice and eggs là đồ ăn sáng → breakfast.

questions.questionText: Don't forget to _____ before school.
questions.explanation: Brush teeth trước khi đi học — thói quen vệ sinh.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete Morning Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word or phrase.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] at six thirty. | I have [1] with my family. | Then I [2] and brush my teeth.
questions.explanation: wake up; breakfast; get dressed — thứ tự buổi sáng.

content.template: I [0] at six thirty. I have [1] with my family. Then I [2] and brush my teeth.

exercises.title: Review: Morning Routine Mix
exercises.instructions: Mixed review from this lesson. Choose carefully.

questions.questionText: Every morning I _____ at six thirty.
questions.explanation: Wake up (thức dậy) — hoạt động đầu tiên trong ngày.

questions.questionText: After you eat in the morning, you _____ for school.
questions.explanation: Get dressed (mặc quần áo) sau bữa sáng.

questions.questionText: The meal you eat before lunch is _____.
questions.explanation: Breakfast (bữa sáng) — bữa trước trưa.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Meals and School Day
lessons.learningObjective: Use lunch, dinner and homework to talk about meals and after-school activities.

exercises.title: Learn: Meals and School
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: At twelve o'clock students eat _____.
questions.explanation: Lunch (bữa trưa) vào giữa trưa ở trường.

questions.questionText: The family eats _____ together at six o'clock.
questions.explanation: Dinner (bữa tối) thường buổi tối.

questions.questionText: After school Minh does his _____.
questions.explanation: Homework (bài tập về nhà) sau giờ học.

questions.questionText: Minh goes to _____ at seven thirty every morning.
questions.explanation: School — đi học buổi sáng (ngữ cảnh Movers).

exercises.title: Practice: Match Meals and Activities
exercises.instructions: Match each word to the correct time or situation.

questions.questionText: Match meals and school-day words.
questions.explanation: Ghép bữa ăn và hoạt động với thời điểm phù hợp.

pairs.right: where you learn in the morning

exercises.title: Check: Meals Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Minh has _____ at school at twelve o'clock.
questions.explanation: Twelve o'clock at school → lunch.

questions.questionText: He always does his _____ before dinner.
questions.explanation: Homework trước bữa tối — theo passage Minh.

questions.questionText: We eat _____ at six in the evening.
questions.explanation: Six in the evening → dinner.

exercises.title: Apply: School Day Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences about Minh's school day.

questions.questionText: Complete: Minh goes to [0] at seven thirty. | He has [1] at twelve. | After school he does his [2].
questions.explanation: school; lunch; homework — ngày đi học của Minh.

content.template: Minh goes to [0] at seven thirty. He has [1] at twelve. After school he does his [2].

exercises.title: Review: Meals and School Mix
exercises.instructions: Mixed review. No hints.

questions.questionText: Students eat _____ at twelve at school.
questions.explanation: Lunch (bữa trưa) — giữa ngày ở trường.

questions.questionText: I do my _____ when I get home from school.
questions.explanation: Homework (bài tập) — sau khi tan học.

questions.questionText: We eat _____ together in the evening.
questions.explanation: Dinner (bữa tối) — buổi tối.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: How Often?
lessons.learningObjective: Use always, sometimes, never and every day to say how often you do things.

exercises.title: Learn: Frequency Words
exercises.instructions: Choose the word that best completes each sentence.

questions.questionText: I _____ brush my teeth in the morning. (100% of the time)
questions.explanation: Always (luôn luôn) = mọi lúc.

questions.questionText: I _____ play football after homework. (not every day)
questions.explanation: Sometimes (đôi khi) = thỉnh thoảng.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ watches TV before homework. (0% of the time)
questions.explanation: Never (không bao giờ) = không lần nào.

questions.questionText: I walk to school _____. (each day)
questions.explanation: Every day (mỗi ngày) = hàng ngày.

exercises.title: Practice: Match How Often
exercises.instructions: Match each adverb to its meaning.

questions.questionText: Match the frequency words.
questions.explanation: Always/sometimes/never/ every day — bốn mức độ khác nhau.

exercises.title: Check: Frequency Challenge
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ does his homework before dinner.
questions.explanation: Trong bài đọc Minh always làm bài trước tối.

questions.questionText: He _____ plays football in the park.
questions.explanation: Passage: sometimes plays football.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Never đứng trước động từ chính watch.

choices.text: I watch never TV before homework.
I never watch TV before homework.
Never I watch TV before homework.

exercises.title: Apply: How Often Do You…?
exercises.instructions: Fill in with always, sometimes or never.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] wake up at six thirty. | I [1] play football after school. | I [2] watch TV before homework.
questions.explanation: always; sometimes; never — mẫu câu thói quen hợp lý.

content.template: I [0] wake up at six thirty. I [1] play football after school. I [2] watch TV before homework.

exercises.title: Review: Frequency Words Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the best frequency word.

questions.questionText: I _____ eat breakfast with my family. (every day)
questions.explanation: Always (luôn luôn) — mỗi ngày đều ăn sáng cùng gia đình.

questions.questionText: She _____ plays football. (about twice a week)
questions.explanation: Sometimes (đôi khi) — không phải mỗi ngày.

questions.questionText: He _____ walks to school on rainy days. (0%)
questions.explanation: Never (không bao giờ) — 0% trên ngày mưa.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Present Simple Habits
lessons.learningObjective: Use present simple to describe daily habits, including third person -s.

exercises.title: Learn: Daily Habits
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct verb form.

questions.questionText: I _____ up at six thirty every day.
questions.explanation: I + wake up (present simple, không -s).

questions.questionText: Minh _____ breakfast with his family.
questions.explanation: Minh (he) → has với third person.

questions.questionText: They _____ to school at seven thirty.
questions.explanation: They + go (plural, không -s).

questions.questionText: She _____ her homework after school.
questions.explanation: She → does (do + es cho third person).

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Habits
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct present simple form.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] up at six. | He [1] breakfast at seven. | We [2] to school together.
questions.explanation: wake; has; go — present simple với I/he/we.

content.template: I [0] up at six. He [1] breakfast at seven. We [2] to school together.

exercises.title: Check: Habits Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ to bed at nine o'clock.
questions.explanation: Minh (he) → goes to bed.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Present simple thói quen — không dùng am/is + V-ing.

choices.text: I am brush my teeth every morning.
I brush my teeth every morning.
I brushing my teeth every morning.

questions.questionText: My friends _____ lunch at school.
questions.explanation: Friends (plural) → have lunch.

exercises.title: Apply: Build Routine Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order.

questions.questionText: Make: I / wake up / at / six thirty / every day.
questions.explanation: I wake up at six thirty every day.

questions.questionText: Make: Minh / has / breakfast / with / his family.
questions.explanation: Minh has breakfast with his family.

exercises.title: Review: Present Simple Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct verb form.

questions.questionText: Every day Linh _____ to school at seven thirty.
questions.explanation: Linh (she) → goes với third person -s.

questions.questionText: We _____ lunch at twelve.
questions.explanation: We (số nhiều) → have, không has.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Present simple thói quen — I do my homework.

choices.text: I do my homework after school.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Adverbs of Frequency
lessons.learningObjective: Place always, sometimes and never correctly before the main verb.

exercises.title: Learn: Adverb Position
exercises.instructions: Choose the sentence with correct adverb position.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Always đứng trước động từ chính do.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Sometimes trước play.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Never trước watch.

choices.text: He watches never TV before homework.
He never watches TV before homework.

questions.questionText: They are _____ hungry before dinner.
questions.explanation: Với be → adverb sau am/is/are: are always.

exercises.title: Practice: Add the Adverb
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the adverb in brackets.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] brush my teeth. (always) | Minh [1] plays football. (sometimes) | She [2] watches TV before homework. (never)
questions.explanation: always; sometimes; never — trước động từ chính.

content.template: I [0] brush my teeth. Minh [1] plays football. She [2] watches TV before homework.

exercises.title: Check: Frequency Grammar
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct sentence.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ forgets to brush his teeth.
questions.explanation: Never = không bao giờ quên → never forgets.

questions.questionText: Which is correct?
questions.explanation: We are always — adverb sau are.

choices.text: We always are tired after school.
We are always tired after school.
We are tired always after school.

questions.questionText: I _____ have rice for breakfast.
questions.explanation: Every day có thể đứng cuối hoặc đầu; ở đây cuối câu.

exercises.title: Apply: Order the Words
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make: I / never / watch / TV / before / homework.
questions.explanation: I never watch TV before homework.

questions.questionText: Make: She / sometimes / plays / football / after / school.
questions.explanation: She sometimes plays football after school.

exercises.title: Review: Adverb Position Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct sentence.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Never đứng trước watch.

choices.text: I eat never dinner before six.
I never eat dinner before six.

questions.questionText: Minh _____ plays football after homework.
questions.explanation: Sometimes — đôi khi chơi, không phải luôn.

questions.questionText: They are _____ late for school.
questions.explanation: Với be → are never (trạng từ sau are).

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Questions About Routines
lessons.learningObjective: Ask and answer What time…? and Do you…? questions about daily routines.

exercises.title: Learn: Routine Questions
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct question word or auxiliary.

questions.questionText: _____ time do you wake up?
questions.explanation: What time — hỏi giờ.

choices.distractorNote: Also possible but What time is standard

questions.questionText: _____ you always brush your teeth?
questions.explanation: Do you — yes/no question present simple.

questions.questionText: _____ does Minh go to school?
questions.explanation: When — hỏi thời điểm (có thể dùng What time).

questions.questionText: _____ she do her homework after school?
questions.explanation: Does + she — third person question.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Questions
exercises.instructions: Fill in the gaps to make questions.

questions.questionText: Complete: [0] time do you have lunch? | [1] you do homework every day? | [2] does Minh go to bed?
questions.explanation: What; Do; When — ba dạng câu hỏi routine.

content.template: [0] time do you have lunch? [1] you do homework every day? [2] does Minh go to bed?

exercises.title: Check: Question Forms
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct question.

questions.questionText: Which question is correct?
questions.explanation: What time do you — đúng trật tự auxiliary.

choices.text: What time do you wake up?
What time do you wakes up?

questions.questionText: Best answer: Do you always eat breakfast?
questions.explanation: Yes, I always eat breakfast — short natural answer.

questions.questionText: Which question is correct?
questions.explanation: Does he — third person với go (không goes trong question).

choices.text: Do he go to bed at nine?
Does he go to bed at nine?
Does he goes to bed at nine?

exercises.title: Apply: Build Questions
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make a question.

questions.questionText: Make: What / time / do / you / have / dinner?
questions.explanation: What time do you have dinner?

questions.questionText: Make: Do / you / sometimes / play / football?
questions.explanation: Do you sometimes play football?

exercises.title: Review: Question Forms Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct question or answer.

questions.questionText: _____ do you go to bed?
questions.explanation: What time — hỏi giờ đi ngủ.

questions.questionText: _____ your brother do homework every day?
questions.explanation: Does + he/she/your brother — third person.

questions.questionText: Best answer: When do you have breakfast?
questions.explanation: At + time — trả lời thời gian tự nhiên.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Read for Gist
lessons.learningObjective: Read a short text about a school day and identify the main idea.

exercises.title: Learn: Minh's School Day
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Answer the questions.

passage.title: Minh's School Day
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. Every day he wakes up at six thirty and has breakfast with his family. Then he gets dressed and brushes his teeth.

At seven thirty Minh goes to school. He has lunch at twelve. After school he always does his homework before dinner. He sometimes plays football in the park.

Minh never watches TV before homework. He eats dinner at six and goes to bed at nine.
passage.imagePrompt: A Vietnamese boy aged nine in a bright bedroom getting ready for school, with a school bag, clock showing morning time, and a family breakfast table in the background; cheerful children's book illustration style.

questions.questionText: Who is the text about?
questions.explanation: The text is about Minh, a nine-year-old boy in Hanoi.

questions.questionText: What is the text mainly about?
questions.explanation: Toàn bài mô tả một ngày đi học của Minh.

exercises.title: Practice: Morning or Evening?
exercises.instructions: Read again. Decide if each activity is mainly morning or evening.

passage.title: Minh's School Day

Minh never watches TV before homework. He eats dinner at six and goes to bed at nine.

questions.questionText: When does Minh wake up?
questions.explanation: He wakes up at six thirty — early morning.

questions.questionText: When does Minh go to bed?
questions.explanation: Goes to bed at nine o'clock — evening.

questions.questionText: Which sentence is NOT true about Minh?
questions.explanation: Minh never watches TV before homework — không xem trước bài.

choices.text: He always watches TV before homework.

questions.questionText: How often does Minh do homework before dinner?

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Find Details
lessons.learningObjective: Find specific times, meals and facts in a short routine text.

exercises.title: Learn: Find the Time
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Find the details.

passage.title: Minh's School Day

At seven thirty Minh goes to school. He has lunch at twelve. After school he always does his homework before dinner. He sometimes plays football in the park.

At seven thirty Minh goes to school. He has lunch at twelve. After school he always does his homework before dinner. He sometimes plays football in the park.

Minh never watches TV before homework. He eats dinner at six and goes to bed at nine.
passage.imagePrompt: A Vietnamese boy aged nine in a bright bedroom getting ready for school, with a school bag, clock showing morning time, and a family breakfast table in the background; cheerful children's book illustration style.

questions.questionText: What time does Minh wake up?
questions.explanation: Wakes up at six thirty — chi tiết buổi sáng.

questions.questionText: Does Minh watch TV before homework?
questions.explanation: Never watches TV before homework — never = không bao giờ.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Order and Connectors
lessons.learningObjective: Follow the order of events in a routine text using then and after.

exercises.title: Learn: First, Then, After
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Answer about order.

passage.title: Minh's School Day
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. Every day he wakes up at six thirty and has breakfast with his family. Then he gets dressed and brushes his teeth.

At seven thirty Minh goes to school. He has lunch at twelve. After school he always does his homework before dinner. He sometimes plays football in the park.

Minh never watches TV before homework. He eats dinner at six and goes to bed at nine.
passage.imagePrompt: A Vietnamese boy aged nine in a bright bedroom getting ready for school, with a school bag, clock showing morning time, and a family breakfast table in the background; cheerful children's book illustration style.

questions.questionText: What does Minh do FIRST in the morning?
questions.explanation: First he wakes up at six thirty.

questions.questionText: What happens AFTER breakfast?
questions.explanation: Then he gets dressed and brushes his teeth.

exercises.title: Practice: Put Events in Order
exercises.instructions: Read again. Choose the correct sequence.

passage.title: Minh's School Day
passage.text:
Minh is nine. He lives in Hanoi. Every day he wakes up at six thirty and has breakfast with his family. Then he gets dressed and brushes his teeth.

At seven thirty Minh goes to school. He has lunch at twelve. After school he always does his homework before dinner. He sometimes plays football in the park.

Minh never watches TV before homework. He eats dinner at six and goes to bed at nine.
passage.imagePrompt: A Vietnamese boy aged nine in a bright bedroom getting ready for school, with a school bag, clock showing morning time, and a family breakfast table in the background; cheerful children's book illustration style.

questions.questionText: Put in order: (1) homework (2) school (3) wake up
questions.explanation: Morning: wake up → school → after school homework.

questions.questionText: Put in order: (1) dinner (2) homework (3) lunch
questions.explanation: Lunch at school → homework after school → dinner at six.

exercises.instructions: Use the passage to answer.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Times in the Morning
lessons.learningObjective: Listen to a morning dialogue and extract times and routine words.

exercises.title: Learn: At Home in the Morning
exercises.instructions: Listen to Minh and his mum. Answer the questions.

script.title: At Home in the Morning
script.setting: A kitchen in the morning, light traffic outside

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Minh, wake up! It's six thirty.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: OK, Mum. I'm awake.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Good. Have your breakfast before you get dressed.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I always have rice and eggs for breakfast.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: Don't forget to brush your teeth.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I never forget. School starts at seven thirty.

lines.speaker: Mum
lines.text: That's right. Leave the house at seven fifteen.

script.audioNotes: Clear mother-and-son dialogue, moderate pace with short pauses. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: What time does Mum wake Minh up?
questions.explanation: Mum says: It's six thirty.

questions.questionText: What does Minh always have for breakfast?
questions.explanation: I always have rice and eggs for breakfast.

exercises.title: Practice: More Morning Details
exercises.instructions: Listen again. Answer the next questions.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: After-School Routine
lessons.learningObjective: Listen for sequence words (first, then) in an after-school conversation.

exercises.title: Learn: After School with Linh
exercises.instructions: Listen to Minh and Linh. Answer the questions.

script.title: After School with Linh

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Hi Minh! What do you do after school?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: I always do my homework first. Then I sometimes play football.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: When do you have dinner?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: At six o'clock. I never watch TV before homework.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Me too. What time do you go to bed?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: At nine. First I read a book, then I go to bed.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Good routine! See you tomorrow.

script.audioNotes: Friendly after-school chat between two children. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does Minh do FIRST after school?
questions.explanation: I always do my homework first.

questions.questionText: When does Minh have dinner?
questions.explanation: At six o'clock.

exercises.title: Practice: Listen for Never
exercises.instructions: Listen again. Focus on frequency words.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Write About Your Morning
lessons.learningObjective: Use frames to write short sentences about morning routines.

exercises.title: Learn: Morning Writing Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences about morning routines.

questions.questionText: Complete: I [0] up at six thirty. | I [1] breakfast at seven. | I [2] brush my teeth before school.
questions.explanation: wake; have; always — khung viết buổi sáng.

content.template: I [0] up at six thirty. I [1] breakfast at seven. I [2] brush my teeth before school.

exercises.title: Practice: Order Morning Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order.

questions.questionText: Make: I / always / have / breakfast / with / my family.
questions.explanation: I always have breakfast with my family.

exercises.title: Check: Write About Your Morning
exercises.instructions: Write a short note about what you do every morning. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a short note to your English teacher about your morning routine. Say when you wake up, what you eat, and one thing you always do.
prompts: What do you have for breakfast?
What do you always do before school?
successCriteria: Mentions wake up time or breakfast
Uses always, sometimes or never at least once
Present simple verbs are mostly correct

modelAnswer.text: I wake up at six thirty every day. I have breakfast with my family. I always brush my teeth and get dressed before school. I leave home at seven fifteen.

grammar.criteria: Uses present simple and adverbs of frequency correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses routine words from the unit (meals, homework, wake up, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical daily order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Sentence
exercises.instructions: Choose the best written sentence for each situation.

questions.questionText: Best sentence about waking up:
questions.explanation: Present simple + time — chuẩn Movers.

choices.text: I am wake up at six thirty.
I wake up at six thirty every day.
I wakes up at six thirty.

questions.questionText: Best sentence with always:
questions.explanation: Always trước brush.

choices.text: I brush always my teeth in the morning.
I always brush my teeth in the morning.
Always I brush my teeth morning.

exercises.title: Review: Morning Writing Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best way to complete each writing frame.

questions.questionText: Complete: Every morning I [0] up early and [1] dressed for school.
questions.explanation: wake; get — ôn khung viết buổi sáng.

content.template: Every morning I [0] up early and [1] dressed for school.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Write About Meals
lessons.learningObjective: Write 2–3 connected sentences about meals and where you eat them.

exercises.title: Learn: Meal Sentences
exercises.instructions: Complete sentences about meals.

questions.questionText: Complete: I have [0] at home. | I have [1] at school at twelve. | We eat [2] at six.
questions.explanation: breakfast; lunch; dinner — ba bữa ăn.

content.template: I have [0] at home. I have [1] at school at twelve. We eat [2] at six.

exercises.title: Practice: Connect Meal Ideas
exercises.instructions: Put words in order to make meal sentences.

questions.questionText: Make: I / have / lunch / at / school / every day.
questions.explanation: I have lunch at school every day.

exercises.title: Check: Write About Your Meals
exercises.instructions: Write 2–3 sentences about your meals. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Write 2–3 sentences about breakfast, lunch and dinner. Say where you have each meal and one food you like.
prompts: Where do you have lunch on school days?
What time is dinner at your home?

modelAnswer.text: I have breakfast at home with my family. At school I have lunch at twelve o'clock. We eat dinner at six. I sometimes help my mother after dinner.

grammar.criteria: Uses present simple and adverbs of frequency correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses routine words from the unit (meals, homework, wake up, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical daily order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Best Meal Message
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short message.

questions.questionText: Best message about lunch:
questions.explanation: Place + time — đủ thông tin Movers.

choices.text: I have lunch at school at twelve o'clock.
I am have lunch at school.

exercises.title: Review: Meals Writing Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the meal writing frames.

questions.questionText: Complete: At home I eat [0]. At school I have [1]. In the evening we have [2] together.
questions.explanation: breakfast; lunch; dinner — ôn bữa ăn.

content.template: At home I eat [0]. At school I have [1]. In the evening we have [2] together.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: My Daily Routine
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short connected message about a full school day.

exercises.title: Learn: Routine Connectors
exercises.instructions: Complete sentences with then and after.

questions.questionText: Complete: I wake up, then I [0] breakfast. [1] school I do homework. Then I [2] to bed.
questions.explanation: have; After; go — liên từ thứ tự.

content.template: I wake up, then I [0] breakfast. [1] school I do homework. Then I [2] to bed.

exercises.title: Practice: Order My Day
exercises.instructions: Put the sentence parts in logical order.

questions.questionText: Make: After school / I / always / do / my homework / before dinner.
questions.explanation: After school I always do my homework before dinner.

exercises.title: Check: My Daily Routine
exercises.instructions: Write about your full school day. Use at least 15 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a short message to a pen friend describing your school day from morning to bedtime. Include homework and one thing you never do.
prompts: What do you do after school?
What time do you go to bed?

modelAnswer.text: Every day I wake up at six thirty and go to school at seven thirty. After school I always do my homework before dinner. I sometimes play football. I never watch TV before homework and I go to bed at nine.

grammar.criteria: Uses present simple and adverbs of frequency correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses routine words from the unit (meals, homework, wake up, etc.).

organization.criteria: Ideas follow a logical daily order or answer all prompts clearly.

taskAchievement.criteria: Meets minimum length and addresses the writing task.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Paragraph Start
exercises.instructions: Choose the best way to start a routine message.

questions.questionText: Best opening for a pen-friend message:
questions.explanation: Every day + wake up — mở đầu rõ chủ đề.

choices.text: Every day I wake up at six thirty and go to school.

questions.questionText: Best sentence with never:
questions.explanation: Never trước watch — đúng ngữ pháp.

choices.text: I watch never TV before homework.
I never watch TV before I do my homework.
Never I watch TV homework before.

exercises.title: Review: Full Day Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete a short routine message frame.

questions.questionText: Complete: Every day I [0] to school at seven thirty. I [1] do homework after school. I [2] watch TV before homework.
questions.explanation: go; always; never — cả ngày học sinh Movers.

content.template: Every day I [0] to school at seven thirty. I [1] do homework after school. I [2] watch TV before homework.

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Talk About Your Morning
lessons.learningObjective: Choose appropriate spoken phrases about morning routines.

exercises.title: Learn: What to Say in the Morning
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say.

questions.questionText: Someone asks: What time do you wake up? You say:
questions.explanation: I wake up at + time — câu trả lời tự nhiên.

choices.text: I wake up at six thirty.
I am wake up six thirty.

questions.questionText: Best answer: What do you have for breakfast?
questions.explanation: I have + food — đầy đủ câu.

exercises.title: Practice: Morning Replies
exercises.instructions: Choose the best reply.

questions.questionText: Do you always brush your teeth? Best answer:
questions.explanation: Yes + always + verb — trả lời đầy đủ.

choices.text: Yes, I always brush my teeth.

questions.questionText: How do you go to school? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I walk / go by bus — câu hoàn chỉnh.

choices.text: I walk to school with my friend.

exercises.title: Check: Talk About Your Morning
exercises.instructions: Answer the questions about your morning routine. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: The examiner asks about your morning habits before school.
content.pictureDescription: Four frames: (1) child waking up with an alarm clock, (2) eating breakfast at a table, (3) brushing teeth in a bathroom, (4) walking to school with a bag.
followUpQuestions: What time do you wake up?
What do you have for breakfast?
Do you always brush your teeth before school?
How do you go to school?
What do you never do in the morning?
suggestedAnswers: I wake up at six thirty.
Yes, I always brush my teeth.
I walk to school with my friend.
I never watch TV in the morning.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Routine words (breakfast, homework, always) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses present simple and frequency adverbs in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses daily routine vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Quick Morning Replies
exercises.instructions: Choose the best short spoken response.

questions.questionText: Mum says: Don't forget your bag! You say:
questions.explanation: OK, I won't forget — phản hồi lịch sự ngắn.

choices.text: OK, Mum. I have my bag.

exercises.title: Review: Morning Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answer.

questions.questionText: What do you never do in the morning? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I never + verb — cấu trúc chuẩn.

choices.text: I never watch TV in the morning.

questions.questionText: Describe getting ready. Best phrase:
questions.explanation: Then I get dressed — mô tả thứ tự.

choices.text: Then I get dressed and brush my teeth.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: How Often?
lessons.learningObjective: Use always, sometimes and never in short spoken answers.

exercises.title: Learn: Say How Often
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answer with a frequency word.

questions.questionText: Do you always do homework? Best answer:
questions.explanation: Yes, I always do my homework after school.

choices.text: Yes, I always do my homework after school.

questions.questionText: Do you sometimes play sport? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I sometimes play football in the park.

choices.text: I sometimes play football in the park.

exercises.title: Practice: Never and Always
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct spoken response.

questions.questionText: Do you watch TV before homework? Best answer:
questions.explanation: No, I never watch TV before homework.

choices.text: No, I never watch TV before homework.

questions.questionText: Does your family always eat dinner together?
questions.explanation: Yes, we always eat dinner at six.

choices.text: Yes, always we eat six dinner.
Yes, we always eat dinner at six.

exercises.title: Check: How Often?
exercises.instructions: Answer using always, sometimes or never. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: The examiner asks how often you do different activities.
content.pictureDescription: A boy doing homework at a desk, playing football in a park, and watching TV — labels show always, sometimes, never.
followUpQuestions: Do you always do your homework after school?
Do you sometimes play sport after homework?
Do you ever watch TV before homework?
Does your family always eat dinner together?
What do you never do on school nights?
suggestedAnswers: Yes, I always do my homework first.
I sometimes play football in the park.
No, I never watch TV before homework.
We always eat dinner at six.
I never go to bed late on school nights.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Routine words (breakfast, homework, always) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses present simple and frequency adverbs in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses daily routine vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Choose the Best Frequency Reply
exercises.instructions: Pick the most natural spoken answer.

questions.questionText: What do you never do on school nights?
questions.explanation: I never go to bed late on school nights.

choices.text: I never go to bed late on school nights.

exercises.title: Review: Frequency Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best answer using always, sometimes or never.

questions.questionText: How often do you brush your teeth?
questions.explanation: I always brush my teeth in the morning and evening.

choices.text: I always brush my teeth twice a day.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Describe Your School Day
lessons.learningObjective: Describe a full school day in short connected spoken answers.

exercises.title: Learn: School Day Phrases
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say about your school day.

questions.questionText: What time do you go to school? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I go to school at seven thirty.

choices.text: I go to school at seven thirty.

questions.questionText: Where do you have lunch? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I have lunch at school.

choices.text: I have lunch at school at twelve.

exercises.title: Practice: After School to Bedtime
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answers about evening routine.

questions.questionText: What do you do after school? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I do my homework and sometimes play with friends.

choices.text: I do my homework first. Then I sometimes play football.

questions.questionText: What time do you go to bed? Best answer:
questions.explanation: I go to bed at nine o'clock.

choices.text: I go to bed at nine o'clock.

exercises.title: Check: Describe Your School Day
exercises.instructions: Describe your day from morning to bedtime. Speak for up to 90 seconds.

content.prompt: Tell the examiner about your typical school day.
content.pictureDescription: A timeline illustration: wake up → school → lunch → homework → dinner → bed, with clock times for a Vietnamese primary student.
followUpQuestions: What time do you go to school?
What do you do after school?
What time do you go to bed?
suggestedAnswers: I go to school at seven thirty.
I have lunch at school at twelve.
I do my homework and sometimes play with friends.
We have dinner at six o'clock.
I go to bed at nine.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Routine words (breakfast, homework, always) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are short but connected; brief pauses are acceptable.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses present simple and frequency adverbs in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses daily routine vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Tell Your Day
exercises.instructions: Choose the best full-day spoken description.

questions.questionText: Best short description of a school day:
questions.explanation: Connected ideas morning → school → homework → bed.

choices.text: I wake up at six thirty, go to school, do homework and go to bed at nine.
I am school every homework bed nine.

exercises.title: Review: School Day Speaking Quiz
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken answer.

questions.questionText: When do you have dinner? Best answer:
questions.explanation: We have dinner at six o'clock.

choices.text: We have dinner at six o'clock.

questions.questionText: Compare habits: I always… / I never… Best pair:
questions.explanation: Always homework; never TV before it — như Minh.

choices.text: I always do homework first. I never watch TV before homework.
